manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: way, person, cause, report, mind, kid, army, middle, president.
Your opponent's team words are: law, doctor, world, child, city, record, gold, fool.
Distractor words are: story, coffee, light, evening, company, hair, ship.
The kill words are: hospital.

message.content:
CLUE: leadership
TARGETS: president, army, person
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue related to some of our team's words: way, person, cause, report, mind, kid, army, middle? Actually "middle" is on list. president.

We need to avoid clue being any of the words in any list (including opponent, distractor, kill). So cannot use "way", "person", etc. Must be a single word.

Goal: maximize number of targets but avoid ambiguity with opponent's words.

We need to list which target words we relate. Probably we can clue "lead" could relate to "person", "army", "president", "cause"? Could also be "lead" ambiguous with "law"? Not in list. "lead" not in opponent list. Could be okay.

But "lead" also could be "kid"? Not.

Another possible clue: "command". Relates to "army", "president", "person", maybe "mind"? Might be ambiguous with "law"? Not.

"policy". Relates to "cause", "president", "report", "army"? Could be ambiguous with "law" (opponent). Might cause opponent guess "law". Better avoid.

We could choose "leadership". But "leadership" contains "lead" but not a list word. Could relate to "president", "army", "person", "middle"? Might be okay.

But we must be careful: opponent's words: law, doctor, world, child, city, record, gold, fool. Avoid clue that could directly hint those.

If clue "president" is a target itself, cannot use.

Maybe clue "center". Could relate to "middle". Also "mind"? No. Could also relate to "army"? Not.

Maybe clue "cause" also is a target, can't use.

We need to pick clue that relates to at least perhaps 3 words to be efficient.

Consider "government". Could relate to "president", "army", "report"? Actually "report" could be a government report. Also "law" is opponent, but "government" could also hint "law". Might be ambiguous.

What about "youth"? Could relate to "kid", "person"? Not.

Clue "strategy". Could relate to "army", "mind", "report", "cause"? Also "law"? Not.

But opponent may guess "doctor"? Not.

Clue "mission". Could relate to "army", "cause", "person", "president"? Could be okay.

But "mission" might also relate to "world"? Not strongly.

Clue "command". Relates to "army", "president (command the nation)", "person", "mind (command your mind)". Might be okay.

Need to list the target words we plan: maybe "army, president, mind". That's three.

Clue "command". Does "command" relate to "mind"? Could be command your mind. Might be a stretch but possible.

Better clue "leadership" relates to "president", "army (leaders)", "person", "mind"? Might be okay.

But we must ensure clue not in any list. "leadership" not in lists.

Let's choose "leadership" and target "president, army, person". That's three.

Will opponent guess any opponent words? Probably not.

Thus answer format:

CLUE: leadership
TARGETS: president, army, person

Make sure formatting correct.

story, way, world, mind, company, hospital, law, light, fool, kid, hair, ship, middle, doctor, city, child, cause, evening, president, report, coffee, record, gold, person, army
